title: Octopus Workflow
description: Octopus workflow YAML schema — 7 node types (bash, python, agent, condition, approval, loop, swarm) with variable pooling, expressions, auto answers, DAG parallel execution, sub-agent definitions, and multi-expert swarm collaboration.

apiVersion.description: Octopus workflow API version (e.g. octopus/v1). Must match octopus/v{number}.

kind.description: File type identifier. Must be 'Workflow' for Octopus workflow YAML files.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this workflow does

model.description: Default AI model for agent nodes (e.g. claude-3-opus, gpt-4o). Can be overridden per node.

engine.description: Default AI provider for agent nodes (e.g. claude). Can be overridden per node.

timeout.description: Global timeout in seconds for the entire workflow

execution_mode.description: Execution mode. 'auto' — nodes without depends_on run concurrently in parallel levels. 'serial' — all nodes execute sequentially, preserving legacy behavior.

max_concurrent.description: Maximum number of nodes that can run concurrently within a single execution level. When unset, all nodes in a level run in parallel without limit. Setting to 1 is equivalent to execution_mode: serial.

variables.description: Initial global variable pool. Values can be any JSON-compatible type (string, number, boolean, array, object). Referenced via $vars.xxx.

auto_answers.description: Global auto-answer rules injected into every agent node prompt. For unattended/CI execution.

pattern.description: Regex or substring pattern to match in agent output

inputs.description: Workflow input parameter definitions. Values are provided at execution time and referenced via $inputs.xxx.

providers.description: Notification provider definitions. Each key is a provider name; each value configures a delivery backend (hermes CLI or HTTP webhook). Used by notify-type hooks.

channels.description: Notification channel profiles. Each key is a channel name referenced by notify hooks; each value binds a channel to a provider and optional target/severity filter.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hooks triggered on workflow and node events. Each event type maps to an array of hook definitions.

nodes.description: Ordered list of workflow nodes (steps). In execution_mode: auto, nodes without depends_on run concurrently; in serial mode, nodes execute sequentially.

ExpertDef.description: A swarm expert definition. Each expert represents a role with a specific perspective that participates in multi-expert collaboration.

role.description: Expert role identifier. Used in MessageBus for from/to routing and in outputs.

agent_file.description: Path to a .md agent definition file. Engine reads content, strips YAML frontmatter, and concatenates with prompt.

prompt.description: Inline prompt for this expert. At least one of agent_file or prompt is required.

perspective.description: Expert's viewpoint angle. Injected into the expert prompt as 'Your perspective: ...'.

task.description: [dispatch mode] Specific task for this expert to execute. Replaces prompt for task-oriented experts.

depends_on.description: [dispatch mode] Expert role IDs that must complete before this expert runs. Builds internal DAG via Kahn's algorithm.

tools.description: Allowed tools for this expert (e.g. Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob).

model.description: Model override for this expert (sonnet, opus, haiku). Defaults to expert_defaults.model.

engine.description: AI provider engine for this expert (e.g. 'claude', 'pi'). Priority: expert.engine → node.engine → workflow.engine → 'claude'.

SubAgentDefinition.description: A sub-agent definition for the Claude Agent SDK 'agents' parameter. Each sub-agent runs within the parent agent node and can execute in parallel (background: true) or sequentially.

description.description: Tells Claude when to invoke this sub-agent

prompt.description: System prompt for the sub-agent. Optional when agent_file is provided — will be appended to agent_file content.

agent_file.description: Path to a .md agent definition file. Engine reads file content, strips YAML frontmatter, and concatenates with prompt. Supports $vars.* substitution.

model.description: Model override for this sub-agent (sonnet, opus, haiku)

skills.description: Skill names to load into this sub-agent

maxTurns.description: Maximum conversation turns for this sub-agent

background.description: If true, this sub-agent runs in non-blocking parallel mode. Other sub-agents can proceed without waiting for it.

effort.description: Reasoning effort level for this sub-agent

Hook.description: A lifecycle hook definition. Can be an agent hook (natural language prompt), a bash hook (shell script), or a notify hook (structured notification via providers/channels).

id.description: Optional identifier for this hook, used in logs and error messages.

type.description: Hook execution type. 'agent' — AI prompt. 'bash' — shell script. 'notify' — structured notification dispatch.

prompt.description: [agent hook] Natural language task description. Variables ($hook.*, $vars.*) are substituted.

bash.description: [bash hook] Shell script body. Variables ($hook.*, $vars.*) are substituted.

timeout.description: Hook timeout in seconds. Default: 60 for bash, 120 for agent.

nodes.description: [on_node_* only] Limit hook to specific node IDs.

condition.description: Expression evaluated before hook execution. If falsy, hook is skipped.

channel.description: [notify hook] Target channel name(s) for notification delivery. Must match a key defined in top-level 'channels'. Supports single channel or array for multi-channel broadcast.

template.description: [notify hook] Message template with severity, title, and optional body. Supports ${var | filter} variable substitution.

on_failure.description: [notify hook] Behavior when notification delivery fails. 'log' — log error and continue. 'retry' — retry per retry config. 'abort' — abort remaining hooks in the chain.

retry.description: [notify hook] Retry configuration for notification delivery. Only effective when on_failure is 'retry'.

NotifyProviderConfig.description: Configuration for a notification delivery provider. Defines the backend type and connection parameters.

type.description: Provider backend type. 'hermes' — CLI-based delivery via hermes send command. 'webhook' — HTTP POST/PUT to a URL with SSRF protection.

timeout.description: Delivery timeout in seconds per attempt.

min_severity.description: Minimum severity threshold for this provider. Messages below this severity are silently dropped.

cli_path.description: [hermes type] Path to the hermes CLI binary. Defaults to 'hermes' in PATH.

url.description: [webhook type] Target URL for HTTP delivery. Subject to SSRF protection (private IPs, localhost, and metadata endpoints are blocked).

method.description: [webhook type] HTTP method for delivery requests.

headers.description: [webhook type] Custom HTTP headers sent with each delivery request. Values support $vars.* substitution.

ChannelProfile.description: A named notification channel that binds a provider to an optional target and severity filter. Referenced by channel name in notify hooks.

provider.description: Provider name — must match a key defined in top-level 'providers'.

target.description: Default delivery target (e.g. 'telegram:chat_id' for hermes, or a URL for webhook). Can be overridden per hook.

url.description: URL override for this channel. Takes precedence over the provider-level url.

min_severity.description: Channel-level severity filter. Messages below this severity are silently dropped. Overrides provider-level min_severity.

NotifyTemplate.description: Notification message template. Supports ${var | filter} variable substitution and {{#if var}}...{{/if}} conditionals (max nesting depth 3).

severity.description: Message severity level. Used for filtering against provider/channel min_severity thresholds.

body.description: Optional message body. Supports the same variable substitution and filter syntax as title. Use \n for line breaks.

NotifyRetry.description: Retry configuration for notification delivery. Only effective when the hook's on_failure is set to 'retry'.

max_attempts.description: Maximum delivery attempts (1–10). Default 1 means no retry.

delay.description: Delay in seconds between retry attempts (0–60).

id.description: Unique node identifier within the workflow. Used for variable references ($node_id.output.xxx) and depends_on.

model.description: AI model override for this node (agent type only)

engine.description: AI provider name for agent nodes. Default: 'claude'. The legacy value 'claude-code' is automatically mapped to 'claude'.

depends_on.description: Node IDs that must complete before this node runs. Enables parallel/DAG execution in auto mode.

execute_when.description: Expression evaluated before execution. If falsy, the node is skipped. Uses the same expression grammar as other fields.

outputs.description: Map variable names to expressions. Evaluated after node succeeds and written into $vars. Use $vars.xxx = expression to assign, or $vars.xxx for read-only mapping.

variables.description: Arbitrary metadata bucket — not used by the engine directly.

bash.description: [bash node] Shell script body. Executed via child_process (bash on Linux/macOS, cmd on Windows). Variables ($vars.xxx, $inputs.xxx) are substituted before execution.

python.description: [python node] Python script body. Executed via python3 child_process. Variables are substituted before execution.

inputs.description: [python / condition node] Key-value inputs injected into the node's evaluation context. For python: passed as environment variables. For condition: referenced via $inputs.xxx in case expressions.

agent.description: [agent node] Agent name to invoke (e.g. skill-searcher, code-reviewer). Must match a registered agent.

skills.description: [agent node] Skill names to load into the agent context

prompt.description: [agent node] The task prompt sent to the AI agent. Variables ($vars.xxx, $node_id.output.xxx) are substituted.

context.description: [agent node] Session context mode. 'new' = start an independent session (stored for resume_from). 'continue' (default) = resume the workflow's global conversation thread.

resume_from.description: [agent node] Explicitly resume a specific context: 'new' node's session. Merges that branch back into the global conversation thread.

goal.description: [agent node — goal mode] High-level goal description. Mutually exclusive with 'prompt'. Engine builds a structured goal prompt with constraints, allowed tools, and context injection.

constraints.description: [agent node — goal mode] List of constraints rendered as bullet points in the goal prompt. Requires 'goal' to be set.

planning.description: [agent node — goal mode] Planning configuration for goal-driven agents.

verify.description: If true, adds 'Verify your result before finishing' instruction

tools.description: Allowed tools rendered in the goal prompt

disallowed_tools.description: Disallowed tools rendered in the goal prompt

agents.description: [agent node] Sub-agent definitions for parallel multi-agent invocation via Claude Agent SDK. Each key is a sub-agent name; each value is a SubAgentDefinition.

auto_answers.description: [agent node] Node-level auto-answer rules. Merged with global auto_answers and compiled into prompt instructions.

cases.description: [condition node] Ordered list of when/then cases. First matching case wins. Use when: "default" for the fallback case.

when.description: Expression evaluated against $vars and $inputs. Truthy → this case matches. Use "default" for fallback.

then.description: Node ID to jump to when this case matches

options.description: [approval node] List of approval choices presented to the user

label.description: Display label shown to the user

value.description: Value written to $vars when this option is selected

approval_timeout.description: [approval node] Timeout in seconds. If no user response within this time, the node fails.

on_reject.description: [approval node] Node ID to jump to when the approval is rejected. If unset, rejection fails the node.

comment_label.description: [approval node] Custom label for the comment input field. When defined, the comment field is displayed above the options.

comment_placeholder.description: [approval node] Custom placeholder text for the comment input field.

while.description: [loop node] Expression evaluated before each iteration. Falsy → exit loop. Defaults to "true" (loop until break or max_iterations).

break_when.description: [loop node] Expression evaluated after each child node. Truthy → exit loop immediately. Checked per child node within an iteration.

continue_when.description: [loop node] Expression evaluated after each child node. Truthy → skip remaining child nodes and start next iteration.

max_iterations.description: [loop node] Safety limit — maximum number of loop iterations

nodes.description: [loop node] Child nodes executed in each iteration

topic.description: [swarm node] The topic/question for all experts to discuss or analyze. Supports $vars.* substitution. Required for swarm nodes.

mode.description: [swarm node] Orchestration mode. review: single-round parallel expert review. debate: multi-round discussion with consensus checks. dispatch: DAG-based task execution with dependencies. swarm: meta-mode where Router auto-selects mode and experts.

experts.description: [swarm node] Predefined expert definitions. Each expert participates in the discussion with their own role and perspective.

dynamic.description: [swarm node] Enable dynamic routing. When true, SwarmRouter auto-selects experts from the role registry (266+ agents) using keyword pre-filter + LLM selection.

max_experts.description: [swarm node] Maximum number of experts the Router can select when dynamic: true. Required when dynamic is enabled.

rounds.description: [swarm node — debate mode] Maximum number of discussion rounds. Default: 3. Ignored in review mode (fixed at 1).

consensus_threshold.description: [swarm node — debate mode] Consensus score (0.0-1.0) threshold for early termination. When Host-assessed consensus >= threshold, discussion ends. Default: 0.7.

budget.description: [swarm node] Token budget limit for the entire swarm execution (all experts + Host). When exhausted, execution stops with status budget_exhausted.

host.description: [swarm node] Host agent definition. The Host synthesizes expert outputs into a final report and (in debate mode) assesses consensus. Default model: opus. Fallback: sonnet → concatenation. Custom host.prompt replaces built-in synthesis instructions.

failure_policy.description: [swarm node] Expert failure handling. fail_fast: stop on first failure (dispatch default). continue_partial: proceed with completed experts (discussion default). retry_failed: retry failed experts.

output_format.description: [swarm node] Host synthesis output format. summary: concise overview. full: comprehensive analysis. structured: JSON with synthesis, experts[], disagreements[], recommendation, confidence.

expert_defaults.description: [swarm node] Default values merged into every expert definition (expert defaults are overridden by expert-level values).

tools.description: Default allowed tools for all experts

disallowed_tools.description: Default disallowed tools for all experts

context_tier.description: [swarm node] Context tier for parameter scaling. '200k' (default): baseline values. '1m': 4× scale for large-context models. Affects token budgets, truncation thresholds, and detail limits.

context_window_rounds.description: [swarm node — debate mode] Number of recent rounds to keep in full text (sliding window). Older rounds are compressed via LLM summarization. Default: 2.

context_token_budget.description: [swarm node] Token budget for discussion context history. When exceeded, emergency truncation activates. Default: 60000 (200k tier) / 240000 (1m tier).

then.description: Agent nodes require at least one of: agent name, prompt, or agents definition.
Swarm nodes require topic and mode. review needs ≥1 expert, debate needs ≥2 experts (unless dynamic: true). dynamic requires max_experts.
